Bapatla

# Bapatla

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Jun 30, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

The Bhavanarayana Swamy temple in Bapatla, is one of the prominent Vaishnava kshetras in Andhra Pradesh. It is a form of Vishnu, in which he is believed to bestow salvation on those who think of him.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!KmTn!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6afaec90-e791-4785-910b-6803759bd799_240x360.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!KmTn!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F6afaec90-e791-4785-910b-6803759bd799_240x360.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Uw3U!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa2bd469a-9028-4926-8947-b6f47c9f6cdc_600x450.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Uw3U!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fa2bd469a-9028-4926-8947-b6f47c9f6cdc_600x450.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

It's believed Narada once had a challenge with Brahma, that he could escape from the Vishnu Maya that covered the world. On his journey he once took a dip in a lake at a place called Sarpapura, and was changed into a beautiful woman. The beautiful lady was married to Nakunda, the young princce of Pithapuram. When her husband was killed in an attack on the kingdom, the grief stricken lady,wanted to end her life. Vishnu appeared as a Brahmin, and asked her to take a dip in the lake.

As the lady took a dip in the lake, she once again became Narada, who was humbled of his arrogance now. On his prayer to Vishnu, he agreed to reside at that place and began to be called as Bhavanarayana. Much like the Pancharamas in Andhra Pradesh, there are the Pancha Bhavanarayana Temples, dedicated to this aspect of Vishnu. Bhavapuri(Bapatla), Swarnapuri( Ponnur), Bhavadevarapalli( now in Krishna district), Sarpavaram( near Kakinada),Pattisema in West Godavari, also Chinnaganjam in Prakasam district.

Bapatla's ancient name was Bhavapuri, after the Bhavanarayana Swamy temple here, that in due course of time became Bhavapatnam, Bhavapatla and later Bapatla. The temple dates to around 594 AD, built by Chola ruler Krimikanta Chola, who also installed 8 Grama Devatas around the temple as guardians of that village- Vallalamma, Kunchalamma, Sankaramma, Singaramma, Dhanakondalamma,Mulakaramma, Nagabhushanamma and Boballamma.

After the decline of the Cholas, the Gajapatis patronized this temple during the 14th century, and later Sri Krishnadeva Raya as well as the later Vijayanagara rulers, under whom the temple received generous grants. Sadly the temple was damaged extensively in raids by the Muslim invaders and later the French, British too. The French especially destroyed all the 8 Grama Devatas around the temple, while the British carried away the Shivalinga nearby.

However thanks to the efforts of Diwan Bahadur Sri Raja Kandregula Jogi Jagannatham of Rajahmundry and Sri Vasireddy Venkatadri Naidu of Guntur, the Bhavanarayanaswamy Temple was once again restored back.

One of the features of the Bhavanarayana Swamy temple is that the sanctum sanctorum cool in the summers inside, and warm during the winter. Also the 2 Dhwaja Stambhas in the temple are in the form of Gajapada( Elephant Foot), very unique.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!In0y!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fc4d68304-6540-49f7-a10f-c52519815d49_360x270.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!cVqd!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F2e341ed0-237c-41f4-b088-3e056c96ed09_360x189.jpeg)

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!9xTi!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F95680eb8-b8b0-4033-94a3-79e4facd6b73_360x234.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!9xTi!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F95680eb8-b8b0-4033-94a3-79e4facd6b73_360x234.jpeg)

Another feature of Bhavanarayana Swamy temple is that the main deity here stands on his toes, as if looking forward eagerly to meet his devotees.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!uckG!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fdf700b4f-89c6-45eb-908b-b7162c1df9c8_494x1046.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!uckG!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fdf700b4f-89c6-45eb-908b-b7162c1df9c8_494x1046.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Also on top of the sanctum sanctorum the top layer is in the form of a fish, and it's believed that touching this would bring good fortune. Also nearby is the Jwala Narasimha temple, that was also built by the Chola ruler. However when fires broke out regularly at nearby village of Karamchedu, the priests said that it was due to the radiance from the murthi.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!n8Oh!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F42ffb206-0735-4d17-9621-2de07ea9aa44_600x450.jpeg)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!n8Oh!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F42ffb206-0735-4d17-9621-2de07ea9aa44_600x450.jpeg)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

In order to cool down the intensity of Jwala Narasimha, the priests advised Krimikanta Chola, to install another shrine of Shanta Keshava Swamy right opposite to pacify him.

Bapatla is one of the major educational centers in Andhra Pradesh, the engineering college is one of the more well known ones. As well as the Acharya N.G.Ranga Agricultural University here.

Also close to Bapatla around 6 km from it, is the Suryalanka Beach, that has a nice resort. Good place to just laze and relax, taking in the views of the sea, as well as the sunrise and sunsets.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!gSLw!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F894bb7f7-5e86-4079-bd97-62056b366382_343x147.jpeg)
